gratefulagents {{ .Chart.AppVersion }} installed as release {{ .Release.Name }} in namespace {{ .Release.Namespace }}.

1. Wait for everything to become ready:

     kubectl -n {{ .Release.Namespace }} get pods -w

2. Retrieve the auto-generated admin password (user: admin):

     kubectl -n {{ .Release.Namespace }} get secret gratefulagents-admin-credentials \
       -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d; echo

3. Open the dashboard (ConnectRPC API + web UI, port 8090):
{{- if eq .Values.dashboard.service.type "ClusterIP" }}

     kubectl -n {{ .Release.Namespace }} port-forward \
       svc/{{ include "gratefulagents.resourceName" (dict "suffix" "controller-manager-dashboard" "context" $) }} 8090:8090

   then browse to http://localhost:8090 and sign in as admin.
{{- else }}

     kubectl -n {{ .Release.Namespace }} get svc \
       {{ include "gratefulagents.resourceName" (dict "suffix" "controller-manager-dashboard" "context" $) }}

   and browse to port 8090 on the address shown, then sign in as admin.
{{- end }}
{{- if .Values.jaeger.enabled }}

4. Traces (Jaeger UI):

     kubectl -n {{ .Release.Namespace }} port-forward \
       svc/{{ include "gratefulagents.resourceName" (dict "suffix" "jaeger" "context" $) }} 16686:16686
{{- end }}

NOTE: agent runs additionally require the agent-sandbox controller
(https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/agent-sandbox) — see the deployment guide
in the gratefulagents README if you have not installed it yet.
